greeting: Afternoon, Haniel — I'm late on purpose-not: the 6 AM cloud run generated your brief but got bounced pushing it (expired GitHub auth). Caught it, regenerated it here. Here's your runway.
hype: Half a day is still a full tank. Two of YOUR products, four clean code blocks, nobody to wait on — close Phase 2 on Module, then push the Takeoff engine. No meetings, no blockers. Go get it.
notice: Tomorrow's 6 AM auto-run needs a 2-min GitHub re-auth first — ping me and I'll walk you through it.

difficulty.note: Comfortable afternoon — 15 min slack. If a block runs long, t16 (door recall) is the safe one to bump to tomorrow.

schedule.title: UX-max pass on module manager (interface-design gate)
Label lane v2: M3 vision for outlined-text firms + callout-vs-room filtering
Door recall round 2 + bi-fold / cased-opening recall

tasks.title: Per-module settings UI (labels, statuses, options)
tasks.notes: Config form per module reading the settings layer — rename labels, define statuses/pipeline stages, edit seedable option lists. Zero-hardcode in practice.

prep.what: Build the config form every module gets — rename labels, define statuses/pipeline stages, edit seedable option lists, all reading the settings layer (zero hardcode).
prep.understand: The settings layer is the spine: each module reads its labels/statuses/options from it, nothing hardcoded. This UI is the super-admin surface that writes to that layer — get the read path confirmed before you build the writer.
approach: Map exactly what a module exposes to settings (labels, statuses, seedable option lists) and confirm the read path is already live
Build ONE config form bound to that schema — sections for labels, pipeline statuses, seedable options (reuse an existing form pattern, don't rebuild it)
Persist + re-seed options on save; prove a renamed label / added status propagates to the module with zero code change
resources: An existing Filament/Inertia form pattern to extend (DRY — don't hand-roll a new one)
A throwaway test module to toggle settings against

tasks.title: UX-max pass on module manager (interface-design gate)
tasks.notes: Run the module manager through interface-design audit until no-notes. UX is a gate, not a phase — principle #1.

prep.what: Run the module manager UI through your interface-design audit until it's no-notes. UX is a gate, not a phase — principle #1.
prep.understand: The manager is the super-admin's daily surface — it has to be obvious and fast, not just functional. 'Functional but clunky' fails the gate.
approach: Walk the full manager flow as a tired admin — count clicks, find dead-ends + dropdown overload
Cut friction: fewer modals/confirms, default behaviors over config knobs, one obvious primary action per screen
Re-audit until no-notes (lean on the ux-architecture-reviewer agent for the brutal pass)
resources: t9's settings UI (audit it in the same pass)

tasks.title: Label lane v2: M3 vision for outlined-text firms + callout-vs-room filtering
tasks.notes: Caught live on the Harborview demo (2026-06-11): rooms show 'unnamed' because PDFTron outlines ALL text (the famous probe finding — 55 live words, zero room labels), so the free live-text lane finds nothing. Holabird proves the lane works (92/109 named in PROD, scale CONFIRMED). v2 = (a) productize the M3 tiles lane (spike run_labels_m3tiles.py, ~4.3 min/sheet, costs money -> the §7a metered-credits event; MINIMAX_AI key must reach the VPS = a secrets decision) as an opt-in 'read labels (AI)' action; (b) callout-vs-room filtering — Holabird shows the noise: general-note paragraphs, UP stair arrows, FEC callouts matched as room names (the R13 documented polish).

prep.what: Ship the AI label-reading lane for firms whose PDFs outline ALL text (so the free live-text lane finds nothing), plus filter callouts/general-notes from real room labels.
prep.understand: PDFTron outlines all text on outlined-firm sheets (Harborview: 55 live words, 0 room labels) so the free lane is blind. The M3 tiles lane WORKS (Holabird: 92/109 named in PROD, scale confirmed) but it costs money + ~4.3 min/sheet → make it opt-in + metered, and the MINIMAX_AI key has to reach the VPS (a secrets decision, not a repo commit).
approach: Productize run_labels_m3tiles.py as an opt-in 'Read labels (AI)' action — NOT default (it's metered + slow)
Wire the §7a metered-credits event so the cost is gated + visible before it runs
Get MINIMAX_AI to the VPS securely (env/secrets path — never in the repo)
Add callout-vs-room filtering: drop general-note paragraphs, UP-stair, and other non-room text so only real room labels survive
resources: Holabird (works) + Harborview (the outlined-text failure) test sets
MINIMAX_AI key + the VPS secrets path

tasks.title: Door recall round 2 + bi-fold / cased-opening recall
tasks.notes: Door recall is THE constraint blocking the global seal relax. Bi-fold detection shipped (scallop-ink-inside-gap); push recall higher via raster-gap detection (symbol-based detectors failed on furniture noise).

prep.what: Push door-detection recall higher (round 2) and add bi-fold + cased-opening recall. Door recall is THE constraint blocking the global seal relax.
prep.understand: Door recall gates the global seal relax — until it's high, you can't relax SEAL_FT. Bi-fold already ships (scallop-ink-inside-gap). Symbol-based detectors already FAILED on furniture noise → don't go back there, go raster-gap.
approach: Profile where current recall misses — bi-fold, cased openings, noisy-furniture false-negatives
Push recall via raster-gap detection (NOT symbol detectors — proven to fail on furniture)
Add cased-opening recall; validate against the door-labeled set; confirm recall climbs without precision collapsing
resources: The door-detection module + the bi-fold scallop-ink-gap code
A door-labeled test set incl. bi-fold + cased openings

waiting_on.title: SMS module (DEFERRED — provider landscape changes too often)
waiting_on.reason: Deferred by decision — do not start until a provider is chosen

waiting_on.title: Production bonus system (target vs actual + quality gate eligibility)
Capacity planning (pipeline load vs team capacity)
Time standard refinement loop (capture actual vs estimated)
